reasoning:
To resolve YES, at least four of the Magnificent Seven must close 50% down from their all-time high before January 1, 2026. Given the August 2025 forecast date, there are only 4.5 months remaining. Currently, Tesla is the only member that has definitively spent significant time 50% below its (2021) all-time high. The other six have all reached fresh all-time highs in late 2024 or 2025. 

Search results indicate that as of August 2025, the MAGS ETF (which tracks these seven companies) is trading around $55–$58, very close to its 52-week high of $59.77. Microsoft even recorded a new all-time high in late July 2025, while others like Apple are only down about 15% year-to-date and Meta is down perhaps 20-25% from its peak. This means at least three more companies would need to experience >50% drawdowns from their recent peaks within the next four months. 

Historically, market crashes of this magnitude (>50% drop) are incredibly rare and usually require a protracted period to play out (e.g., the Dot-com crash took ~2 years, the 2008 financial crisis took ~18 months). Even the 2020 COVID panic only induced ~35% drawdowns for the broader market in a one-month span. Since the stocks are currently quite far from the -50% threshold, it would take an unprecedented, cataclysmic economic event or an instantaneous evaporation of the AI industry to trigger this condition within the remaining time. I am setting the probability at the minimum threshold.
